chapter_title: Regul. of Outdoor Signs on Rural Roads
text:
(a) A county prohibition or regulation adopted under Section 394.061 (Off-premise Portable Signs) prevails over a state law or rule if there is a conflict.

(b) A municipal sign ordinance that has been extended to territory in the municipality’s extraterritorial jurisdiction under Section 216.902 (Regulation of Outdoor Signs in Municipality’s Extraterritorial Jurisdiction) , Local Government Code, prevails in that territory over a county prohibition or regulation adopted under Section 394.061 (Off-premise Portable Signs) if there is a conflict.
markdown:
# Transp. Code
## Title 6
### Chapter 394. Regul. of Outdoor Signs on Rural Roads
##### § 394.062 — Conflict with Other Laws

(a) A county prohibition or regulation adopted under Section 394.061 (Off-premise Portable Signs) prevails over a state law or rule if there is a conflict.

(b) A municipal sign ordinance that has been extended to territory in the municipality’s extraterritorial jurisdiction under Section 216.902 (Regulation of Outdoor Signs in Municipality’s Extraterritorial Jurisdiction) , Local Government Code, prevails in that territory over a county prohibition or regulation adopted under Section 394.061 (Off-premise Portable Signs) if there is a conflict.

*Acts 1995, 74th Leg., ch. 165, Sec. 1, eff. Sept. 1, 1995.*

subsections.label: a
subsections.text: A county prohibition or regulation adopted under Section 394.061 (Off-premise Portable Signs) prevails over a state law or rule if there is a conflict.

subsections.label: b
subsections.text: A municipal sign ordinance that has been extended to territory in the municipality’s extraterritorial jurisdiction under Section 216.902 (Regulation of Outdoor Signs in Municipality’s Extraterritorial Jurisdiction) , Local Government Code, prevails in that territory over a county prohibition or regulation adopted under Section 394.061 (Off-premise Portable Signs) if there is a conflict.

history.raw: Acts 1995, 74th Leg., ch. 165, Sec. 1, eff. Sept. 1, 1995.
